title: Nelipus bifida
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Nelipus bifida". However, I can suggest that you might be referring to "Nelumbo nucifera" or "Nelumbo lutea", also known as the American Lotus or Nelumbo. If that is the case, here is a paragraph about Nelumbo nucifera:

Nelumbo nucifera is a aquatic plant species in the family Nelumbonaceae, also known as the lotus or sacred lotus, with its rhizomes and seeds used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Nelumbo nucifera include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and regulate blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Nelumbo nucifera include alkaloids, saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nelumbo nucifera include tea, capsules, and extracts, which are used to support weight loss, improve digestion, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Nelipus bifida is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Africa, specifically in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Nelipus bifida is further divided into the genus Nelipus and the species N. bifida. The plant is also known as the "bifid Nelipus" or "two-parted Nelipus." Nelipus bifida belongs to the kingdom Plantae, which includes all types of plants.
